title: The Name of the Wind
author: Patrick Rothfuss
description: A gifted young man grows up to become the most notorious wizard his world has ever seen, recounting his journey from tragedy to arcane mastery at a legendary university of magic.

title: A Game of Thrones
author: George R. R. Martin
description: Noble houses vie for control of an iron throne amid political intrigue, betrayal, and looming supernatural threats in a sprawling medieval fantasy world of knights and dragons.

title: The Way of Kings
author: Brandon Sanderson
description: On a storm-wracked world, warriors bound by ancient oaths wield magical blades as a broken kingdom teeters on the edge of a war that could unmake reality itself.

title: The Fifth Season
author: N. K. Jemisin
description: In a land plagued by apocalyptic earthquakes, people who can control geological forces are feared and enslaved, as a mother searches for her daughter across a dying continent.

title: The Hobbit
author: J. R. R. Tolkien
description: A comfortable hobbit is swept into an adventure with a band of dwarves to reclaim their mountain home from a dragon, discovering courage and a magic ring along the way.

title: Mistborn: The Final Empire
author: Brandon Sanderson
description: In a world of ash and mist ruled by an immortal tyrant, a street thief discovers she can fuel magic by swallowing metals and joins a crew plotting an impossible rebellion.

title: The Lies of Locke Lamora
author: Scott Lynch
description: A band of elaborate con artists schemes to swindle the nobility of a canal-laced city, until a mysterious figure threatens the entire criminal underworld.

title: Assassin's Apprentice
author: Robin Hobb
description: The royal bastard of a fading kingdom is secretly trained as an assassin, navigating court politics and a rare magical bond with animals as enemies close in.

title: Dune
author: Frank Herbert
description: On a desert planet that is the sole source of the universe's most valuable substance, a young heir navigates prophecy, ecology, and interstellar war to fulfill his destiny.

title: The Three-Body Problem
author: Liu Cixin
description: A secret military project makes contact with an alien civilization from a chaotic star system, setting humanity on a collision course across light-years and centuries.

title: Project Hail Mary
author: Andy Weir
description: A lone astronaut wakes with amnesia aboard a spacecraft and must use science and ingenuity to solve an extinction-level threat to the sun, with an unexpected ally.

title: Neuromancer
author: William Gibson
description: A washed-up computer hacker is hired for one last job jacking into cyberspace, entangling him with artificial intelligences in a neon-lit future of corporations and crime.

title: The Left Hand of Darkness
author: Ursula K. Le Guin
description: An envoy to an icy planet whose inhabitants have no fixed gender must overcome cultural misunderstanding and betrayal to bring their world into a galactic alliance.

title: Foundation
author: Isaac Asimov
description: A mathematician predicts the fall of a galactic empire and founds a colony to preserve knowledge and shorten the coming dark age across a thousand years of history.

title: Snow Crash
author: Neal Stephenson
description: A pizza-delivering hacker and a teenage skateboard courier race through a fractured near-future America and a virtual metaverse to stop a mind-shattering digital drug.

title: Children of Time
author: Adrian Tchaikovsky
description: The last remnants of humanity flee a dying Earth toward a terraformed world where an uplift experiment has produced an unexpected and rapidly evolving new civilization.

title: Gone Girl
author: Gillian Flynn
description: When a woman vanishes on her fifth wedding anniversary, her husband becomes the prime suspect, and a twisting narrative unravels the dark secrets of their marriage.

title: The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo
author: Stieg Larsson
description: A disgraced journalist and a brilliant, troubled hacker investigate a decades-old disappearance that exposes a wealthy family's buried crimes and violence.

title: The Silent Patient
author: Alex Michaelides
description: A psychotherapist becomes obsessed with treating a painter who shot her husband and then never spoke again, uncovering a shocking truth behind her silence.

title: And Then There Were None
author: Agatha Christie
description: Ten strangers are lured to an isolated island where they are killed off one by one, each death matching a sinister nursery rhyme, with the murderer among them.

title: The Da Vinci Code
author: Dan Brown
description: A symbologist races across Europe to solve a murder at the Louvre, decoding clues hidden in famous artworks that point to a centuries-old religious conspiracy.

title: Big Little Lies
author: Liane Moriarty
description: Three mothers in a seaside town find their seemingly perfect lives unraveling toward a death at a school fundraiser, exposing secrets, lies, and domestic violence.

title: In the Woods
author: Tana French
description: A detective with a buried childhood trauma investigates the murder of a girl in the same woods where his friends vanished decades earlier, blurring past and present.

title: The Reversal
author: Michael Connelly
description: A defense attorney switches sides to prosecute a convicted killer granted a retrial, working with a detective as courtroom maneuvering turns deadly.

title: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen
description: A spirited young woman clashes with a proud, wealthy gentleman in Georgian England, as misjudgments and social expectations slowly give way to mutual love.

title: The Hating Game
author: Sally Thorne
description: Two executive assistants who despise each other are locked in a fierce competition for the same promotion, only to discover their rivalry hides a powerful attraction.

title: Outlander
author: Diana Gabaldon
description: A World War II nurse is mysteriously swept back to eighteenth-century Scotland, where she is torn between her own time and a passionate love for a Highland warrior.

title: It Ends with Us
author: Colleen Hoover
description: A woman building a new life falls for a charming neurosurgeon, but the reappearance of her first love forces her to confront painful truths about the relationship.

title: Beach Read
author: Emily Henry
description: A romance writer and a literary novelist, stuck as neighbors for a summer, challenge each other to swap genres and unexpectedly fall for one another.

title: Me Before You
author: Jojo Moyes
description: A small-town woman takes a job caring for a paralyzed former businessman, and their unlikely bond blossoms into a love that changes both their lives forever.

title: The Notebook
author: Nicholas Sparks
description: An elderly man reads to a woman from a notebook recounting the enduring love story of a young couple separated by class and war in the American South.

title: Red, White & Royal Blue
author: Casey McQuiston
description: The son of the U.S. president and a British prince turn a staged truce into a secret transatlantic romance that could upend an election and two nations.

title: The Great Gatsby
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
description: A mysterious millionaire throws lavish parties in pursuit of a lost love, as a young narrator witnesses the glittering excess and hollow disillusion of the Jazz Age.

title: Beloved
author: Toni Morrison
description: A formerly enslaved woman is haunted, literally and figuratively, by the trauma of her past and the ghost of the daughter she lost, in a searing meditation on memory.

title: Never Let Me Go
author: Kazuo Ishiguro
description: Three friends raised at a secluded English boarding school gradually uncover the quiet, devastating truth about the purpose of their sheltered existence.

title: A Little Life
author: Hanya Yanagihara
description: Four college friends navigate decades of ambition and heartbreak in New York, centered on one man whose hidden past casts a long shadow over his life.

title: The Road
author: Cormac McCarthy
description: A father and son journey through a scorched, post-apocalyptic landscape, clinging to each other and a fragile hope in a spare, haunting portrait of love and survival.

title: Normal People
author: Sally Rooney
description: Two Irish students drift in and out of each other's lives over years, their intense, halting relationship shaped by class, miscommunication, and quiet longing.

title: Cloud Atlas
author: David Mitchell
description: Six nested stories spanning centuries and genres echo one another across time, tracing how individual acts of cruelty and kindness ripple through human history.

title: The Remains of the Day
author: Kazuo Ishiguro
description: An aging English butler reflects on decades of loyal service and a love left unspoken, slowly confronting the personal cost of his lifelong devotion to duty.

title: Sapiens
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: A sweeping account of how Homo sapiens came to dominate the planet, tracing the cognitive, agricultural, and scientific revolutions that shaped human society.

title: A Brief History of Time
author: Stephen Hawking
description: A landmark popular-science explanation of cosmology, from the big bang and black holes to the nature of time, made accessible to general readers.

title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
author: Daniel Kahneman
description: A Nobel laureate reveals the two systems that drive how we think, exposing the biases and errors that shape human judgment and decision-making.

title: The Selfish Gene
author: Richard Dawkins
description: A provocative reframing of evolution from the perspective of genes, arguing that natural selection acts to propagate the units of heredity themselves.

title: Cosmos
author: Carl Sagan
description: A lyrical tour of the universe and humanity's place in it, weaving astronomy, history, and philosophy into a celebration of scientific discovery.

title: The Immortal Life of Henrietta Lacks
author: Rebecca Skloot
description: The story of a poor Black woman whose cancer cells, taken without consent, became one of medicine's most important tools, raising deep questions about ethics.

title: Guns, Germs, and Steel
author: Jared Diamond
description: An ambitious argument that geography and environment, rather than race, explain why some societies developed technology and conquered others across history.

title: The Gene
author: Siddhartha Mukherjee
description: A sweeping history of the gene as a scientific idea, from Mendel's peas to modern genetic engineering, and the moral questions the science now forces upon us.
